questions.question: Who was the prophet that fought against the prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel?
Which prophet announced the prophecy of the birth of a child with descriptive names indicating his future abilities, such as 'Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace'?
What is the meaning of the prophet Hosea's name as expressed in his book?
Which prophet sharply criticized King David following the sin of Bathsheba?
Which of the minor prophets, who was a shepherd by profession, expressed prophecies with social criticism against social injustices throughout Israel?
Who was the prophet that continued Elijah's path after his ascension to heaven?
Which prophet became famous for his amazing visions, especially the vision of the dry bones?
Which prophet is known for his prophecy about the birth of the Messiah from the city of Bethlehem, considered one of the main prophecies of the period?
Which prophet was sent to the city of Nineveh to call for repentance, tried to flee his mission but eventually returned and fulfilled it?
Which prophet foretold the destruction of Jerusalem and dealt with the people's grief?
Which Roman ruler was assassinated in the Senate in 44 BCE?
Which Roman structure is famous for events such as gladiator fights?
What was the main form of government in Rome before the imperial period?
Who is considered the first emperor of Rome?
What was one of the foundations of government that led to the expansion of the Roman Empire?
choices: An economy based solely on maritime trade
Exclusive religious connection with the gods

questions.question: Which of the following was not considered a Roman emperor?
Which battle is considered the turning point that ended the Roman civil wars and ensured Augustus' rise to power?
What was the importance of the Roman Forum?
choices: Residential area for the elite only
Designated area for performances and entertainment

questions.question: Which Roman road was built as a main route linking cities of the empire and is considered one of the engineering achievements of the period?
What was the role of consuls in the Roman Republic?
choices: Only military leaders without political powers
Political leaders with extensive powers over the state command

questions.question: In what year did the Battle of Stamford Bridge take place?
Which ruler is known for establishing a unified legal system in England and for the signing of the Magna Carta?
What was the name of the knights' order established to protect pilgrims to Jerusalem?
Which deadly plague spread across Europe in the 14th century and changed society's face?
Which breakthrough weapon changed the rules of warfare at the end of the Middle Ages?
What was the role of the 'seigneur' in the feudal system?
choices: Lord of the estate who granted land to vassals

questions.question: Which famous fortress located on the coast of France is considered one of the most impressive medieval buildings?
Who was the French female warrior considered a national heroine and was burned at the stake?
What was the primary language of science and scholarship in medieval Europe?
Which architectural style is characterized by pointed arches, flying buttresses, and large stained glass windows?
Which treaty marked the end of the Napoleonic Wars in 1815?
Which battle is considered Napoleon’s final defeat?
Who led the British forces against Napoleon at the Battle of Waterloo?
Which island was Napoleon exiled to first after his abdication in 1814?
What was the name of the military code established by Napoleon that influenced many modern legal systems?
What was the name of the alliance formed by Britain, Russia, Austria, and Prussia to defeat Napoleon after his return from Elba?
What strategy did the Russians use to weaken Napoleon’s army during the invasion of 1812?
What title did Napoleon take after becoming ruler of France in 1804?
Which major naval battle in 1805 prevented Napoleon from invading Britain?
What was the name of Napoleon’s return from exile in 1815, which lasted 100 days?
